```python
# Extract counts for each qubit
qubit_counts = list(counts.values())

# Check if there is at least one qubit that is always observed as a single value
always_single_value = False
for qubit in qubit_counts:
    if len(qubit) == 1:
        always_single_value = True
        break
assert always_single_value

# Check if there is at least one qubit that is observed as both 0 and 1
both_values_observed = False
for qubit in qubit_counts:
    if len(qubit) == 2:
        both_values_observed = True
        break
assert both_values_observed

# Extract counts for each qubit across all shots
qubit_counts = {i: [counts.get(f"{i}", 0) for _ in range(shots)] for i in range(5)}

# Check if there's at least one qubit always observed as a single value
always_single_value = False
for counts_list in qubit_counts.values():
    if len(set(counts_list)) == 1 and counts_list[0] > 0:
        always_single_value = True
        break

# Check if there's at least one qubit observed as both 0 and 1
both_values_observed = False
for counts_list in qubit_counts.values():
    if len(set(counts_list)) == 2 and 0 in counts_list and counts_list.count(0) > 0 and 1 in counts_list and counts_list.count(1) > 0:
        both_values_observed = True
        break

assert always_single_value and both_values_observed
```

        break

```python
# Extract the counts for each qubit from the measurement results
qubit_counts = [{bit: counts.get(bit, 0) for bit in ['0', '1']} for _ in range(5)]

# Check for at least one qubit that is always observed as a single value
always_single = any(len(set(counts.values())) == 1 for counts in qubit_counts)
assert always_single, "No qubit is always observed as a single value"

# Check for at least one qubit that is observed as both 0 and 1
both_values = any(len(counts.values()) == 2 for counts in qubit_counts)
assert both_values, "No qubit is observed as both 0 and 1"
```

python
all_zeros = [qubit for qubit, outcomes in counts.items() if len(outcomes) == 1 and list(outcomes.keys())[0] == 0]
all_ones = [qubit for qubit, outcomes in counts.items() if len(outcomes) == 1 and list(outcomes.keys())[0] == 1]
mixed_outcomes = [qubit for qubit, outcomes in counts.items() if len(outcomes) > 1]

assert len(all_zeros) + len(all_ones) > 0, "No qubit is always observed as a single value across all shots"
assert len(mixed_outcomes) > 0, "No qubit is observed as both 0 and 1 across the shots"

```python
qubit_states = list(counts.values())
always_single_value_qubits = 0
both_values_qubits = 0

for qubit_state in qubit_states:
    if len(qubit_state) == 1:
        always_single_value_qubits += 1
    if len(qubit_state) > 1:
        both_values_qubits += 1

assert always_single_value_qubits >= 1, "No qubit is always observed as a single value"
assert both_values_qubits >= 1, "No qubit is observed as both 0 and 1"
```

always_observed_qubit = None
probabilistic_qubit = None

for qubit in range(len(counts)):

    if len(outcomes) == 1:

        break

assert probabilistic_qubit is not None, "No qubit is observed as both 0 and 1 across shots"

```python
qubit_values = list(counts.values())
always_single_value = False
has_both_values = False

for qubit in range(len(qubit_values[0])):
    qubit_counts = [counts[key][qubit] for key in counts]
    if len(set(qubit_counts)) == 1:
        always_single_value = True
    if len(set(qubit_counts)) == 2 and set(qubit_counts) == {0, 1}:
        has_both_values = True

assert always_single_value, "No qubit is always observed as a single value across all shots"
assert has_both_values, "No qubit is observed as both 0 and 1 across the shots"
```
